title: Pearson Edexcel International GCSE (9–1) Biology Paper 1B - Nov 2025
subject: Biology

sub_questions.ms_text: Correct Answer: D (S) - The nucleus is labelled S.
Correct Answer: B (Q) - The cytoplasm is labelled Q.
Correct Answer: D (S) - The nucleus contains chromosomes.
1 mark for correct measurement (43-44mm or 4.3-4.4cm). 1 mark for correct calculation (e.g. 43000 / 65 = 660um). Allow range 660-677um.

Any three from: Amoeba has nucleus, Amoeba has cytoplasm, Amoeba has organelles (e.g. mitochondrion), Amoeba has cell membrane, Virus has protein coat/capsid/envelope, Amoeba has chromosomes/DNA/RNA while virus has only DNA or RNA.
The net movement of particles from an area of high concentration to an area of low concentration (down a concentration gradient).
Time taken for the litmus paper to change colour.
Line graph: Axes correctly labelled with units, linear scale, points correctly plotted, separate lines for 1 and 3 drops, lines joined with straight ruler lines.
Increasing concentration (more drops) increases the rate of diffusion, so the time taken for colour change decreases.
Repeat the investigation and calculate a mean.
Use a water bath to maintain different temperatures, keep concentration (drops) the same, measure time at each temp.
To show that starch was initially present / to compare results after destarching / reference to control.
The leaf in the flask remains brown/orange (no starch) because the soda lime removed CO2, preventing photosynthesis.
Species of seeds / age of seeds / mass of seeds.
